title: mentha arvensis var. pumila
intro: Mentha arvensis var. pumila, also known as dwarf pennyroyal or lesser wild mint, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to Europe and Asia. It is a member of the mint family and is characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as a natural insect repellent. In herbal preparations, Mentha arvensis var. pumila is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils to promote relaxation, improve respiratory health, and support menstrual health.
